name: Potato, Egg, and Hominy Breakfast
recipeIngredient: 1 can hominy grits, rinsed and drained
1/2 can tomatoes with green chilis, with juice (e.g., Rotel)
1/2 cup Egg Beaters 99% egg substitute, lightly beaten with splash of water or nonfat milk
1/2 package hashed brown potatoes, frozen, cubed style
red and green bell peppers – chopped; optional
recipeInstructions:
1. Use the cubed style frozen hash browns, not the shredded ones. I used Ore-Ida's "Potatoes O'Brien" that has peppers and onions.
- If you are not using frozen hash browns that already have peppers and onions, saute them prior to beginning the potatoes.
2. Preheat nonstick skillet.
3. Add frozen hash browns and seasonings to the skillet. When the potatoes are mostly cooked, add the Rotel tomatoes and drained hominy.
4. Cook until heated through.
5. Make a "crater" in the center of the pan by pushing everything to the side. Pour the lightly beaten Egg Beaters into the hole.
6. Let them begin to set, and gently drag a spatula across the bottom of the pan, allowing the unset eggs to flow to the bottom.
7. When the eggs are fully cooked, gently stir the other ingredients back into the eggs. Be careful not to break the eggs up too much so they don't completely disappear.
8. Note: The eggs may be "scrambled" in a separate pan and then folded into the hominy, potato, and tomato mixture.
9. Lowfat vegetarian sausage patties or links may be precooked, crumbled, and added to the mixture (I liked it with the "sausage" added; my husband preferred his sausage on the side).
